exam.name: Special Education: Core Knowledge and Mild to Moderate Applications
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for prospective special education teachers, mapping the official syllabus across learner development, learning environments, instruction, assessment, and professional responsibilities.
exam.hero_headline: Pass Praxis 5543 with prep mapped directly to the official objectives
exam.hero_subheadline: Study development, instruction, and assessment for mild to moderate applications in focused 15-minute topics aligned to the ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis special education prep either lacks depth on mild to moderate applications or provides too much generic information without aligning to the exact competencies the exam tests.
exam.companion_solution: We translate the official ETS syllabus into a clear, retention-focused learning map with manageable 15-minute notes covering core knowledge and mild to moderate applications.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring special education teacher preparing for selected-response and constructed-response items focused on mild to moderate disabilities. Needs practical strategies mixed with foundational legal and developmental frameworks.

sections.title: Development and Characteristics of Learners
sections.description: Foundational knowledge regarding human development, behavior, and the various characteristics defining specific disabilities and their impacts.

topics.title: Human Development and Behavior
topics.description: Fundamentals of typical human development and recognizing significant differences indicating atypical development.
learning_objectives: Identify stages of typical human development.
Recognize signs of atypical development across various domains.
Understand behavioral theories relevant to child development.

topics.title: Theoretical Approaches to Learning and Motivation
topics.description: Exploration of established theoretical approaches that guide student learning and motivation in special education.
learning_objectives: Compare theoretical approaches to student learning.
Apply theories of motivation to support students with exceptionalities.

topics.title: Basic Characteristics of Major Disability Categories
topics.description: Understanding the defining factors and basic characteristics of each major IDEA disability category.
learning_objectives: Identify basic characteristics for each major IDEA disability category.
Differentiate between developmental delay and specific disabilities.

topics.title: Impact of Disabilities Across the Life Span
topics.description: The ongoing effects of disabilities on individuals, their families, and society from childhood through adulthood.
learning_objectives: Analyze the impact of disabilities on individuals' daily lives.
Understand the effects of disabilities on family dynamics and societal integration.

topics.title: Impact of Language, Cultural, and Gender Differences
topics.description: How cultural, linguistic, and gender differences influence the disability identification process.
learning_objectives: Evaluate how language and cultural differences affect the identification process.
Distinguish between language acquisition processes and learning disabilities.

topics.title: Co-occurring Conditions
topics.description: Recognizing and accommodating individuals who have multiple, co-existing conditions or disabilities.
learning_objectives: Identify common co-occurring conditions (e.g., SLD and ADHD).
Understand how multiple conditions compound educational challenges.

topics.title: Family Systems and Development
topics.description: The contribution of family systems to the development and achievement of individuals with disabilities.
learning_objectives: Explain the role of family systems in supporting individuals with disabilities.
Identify strategies to involve families in the educational process.

topics.title: Environmental and Societal Influences
topics.description: How the broader environment and societal factors affect student development and achievement.
learning_objectives: Analyze how environmental factors impact student achievement.
Understand societal barriers and supports for students with disabilities.

sections.title: Planning and the Learning Environment
sections.description: Skills and knowledge required for creating effective lesson plans, establishing measurable goals, and fostering a safe, supportive classroom.

topics.title: Characteristics of Good Lesson Plans
topics.description: Fundamental qualities that define a well-structured and engaging lesson plan for diverse learners.
learning_objectives: Identify the key components of effective lesson planning.
Design lessons that address the diverse needs of learners.

topics.title: Basic Elements of Effective Lesson Plans
topics.description: The essential structural elements, pacing, and sequencing needed for successful instruction.
learning_objectives: Incorporate evidence-based elements into instructional planning.
Sequence instruction logically for students with mild to moderate disabilities.

topics.title: Measurable and Appropriately Challenging Objectives
topics.description: Writing and aligning learning objectives that are measurable and appropriately difficult.
learning_objectives: Write SMART (Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant, Time-bound) learning objectives.
Align objectives with general education standards and individual student needs.

topics.title: Means of Providing Access to the Curriculum
topics.description: Strategies and adaptations to ensure students with disabilities have access to the general education curriculum.
learning_objectives: Apply Universal Design for Learning (UDL) principles.
Adapt general education curriculum materials to accommodate diverse learners.

topics.title: Organizing the Learning Environment
topics.description: Structuring the physical space and resources to promote accessibility and engagement.
learning_objectives: Design physical spaces to enhance learning and accessibility.
Establish structured routines to promote student engagement.

topics.title: Understanding How to Manage Student Behavior
topics.description: Applying behavioral approaches to develop proactive and responsive student behavior management plans.
learning_objectives: Implement proactive classroom management strategies.
Apply principles of applied behavior analysis (ABA) in the classroom.

topics.title: Design and Maintenance of Safe, Supportive Classrooms
topics.description: Creating an inclusive, emotionally safe, and supportive classroom climate that promotes achievement.
learning_objectives: Foster a classroom climate of respect and inclusion.
Mitigate behavioral triggers by promoting a supportive environment.

sections.title: Instruction
sections.description: Implementation of appropriate instructional strategies, research-based interventions, and assistive technology to ensure student success.

topics.title: Age- and Ability-Appropriate Strategies
topics.description: Selecting and applying instructional techniques suitable for varying developmental ages and abilities.
learning_objectives: Select instructional techniques tailored to student age and developmental level.
Differentiate instruction to address various learning profiles.

topics.title: Instructional Strategies for Different Group Sizes
topics.description: Adapting strategies for one-to-one, small group, and large group instructional settings to ensure success.
learning_objectives: Design instruction for 1-to-1, small group, and large group settings.
Implement peer tutoring and cooperative learning models.

topics.title: Maintenance and Generalization of Concepts
topics.description: Techniques that facilitate students' ability to maintain learned skills over time and generalize them to new contexts.
learning_objectives: Embed strategies to help students maintain learned skills over time.
Promote the transfer of skills to new and varied contexts.

topics.title: Selection and Implementation of Research-Based Interventions
topics.description: Identifying and delivering evidence-based practices and interventions for individual students.
learning_objectives: Identify and select validated, research-based interventions.
Implement multi-tiered systems of support (MTSS/RTI) effectively.

topics.title: Supplementary and Functional Curriculum
topics.description: Implementing curricula focused on life skills, independence, and specific functional deficits.
learning_objectives: Integrate functional life skills training into academic instruction.
Develop supplementary curricula to address specific functional deficits.

topics.title: Options for Assistive Technology
topics.description: Identifying and integrating appropriate assistive technologies to support student learning.
learning_objectives: Identify low-tech, mid-tech, and high-tech assistive technologies.
Evaluate technology options to meet specific student accommodations.

topics.title: Instructional Strategies Supporting Transition Goals
topics.description: Preparing students for post-secondary outcomes through transition-focused instructional strategies.
learning_objectives: Develop instructional activities that align with post-secondary transition goals.
Prepare students for independent living and employment.

topics.title: Preventive Strategies for At-Risk Learners
topics.description: Applying strategies to prevent academic and behavioral failures in at-risk student populations.
learning_objectives: Implement tier-1 and tier-2 preventive supports for at-risk students.
Identify warning signs and deploy early intervention strategies.

sections.title: Assessment
sections.description: Knowledge and application of various assessment types, interpreting results, and using data to guide instruction.

topics.title: Evidence-Based Assessments
topics.description: Identifying and utilizing assessment methods that are proven to be effective and appropriate.
learning_objectives: Select appropriate, evidence-based assessment tools.
Ensure assessments are valid and reliable for the target population.

topics.title: Defining and Using Various Assessments
topics.description: Understanding the definitions and applications of formal, informal, formative, and summative assessments.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between formative, summative, formal, and informal assessments.
Utilize curriculum-based measurement (CBM) in the classroom.

topics.title: Interpreting Assessment Results
topics.description: Analyzing data derived from assessments to understand student strengths and weaknesses.
learning_objectives: Analyze standardized test scores and informal data.
Identify student strengths and weaknesses from evaluation reports.

topics.title: Understanding and Using Results of Assessments
topics.description: Translating assessment interpretations into actionable instructional adjustments and IEP goals.
learning_objectives: Translate assessment data into measurable IEP goals.
Use ongoing assessment data to adjust and individualize instruction.

sections.title: Foundations and Professional Responsibilities
sections.description: Understanding the legal requirements, federal guidelines, professional roles, and ethical obligations in special education.

topics.title: Federal Definitions
topics.description: Reviewing standard terminology and disability classifications established under federal law.
learning_objectives: Define special education terminology according to IDEA.
Understand the criteria for each disability classification under federal law.

topics.title: Pre-Referral, Referral, and Identification
topics.description: The sequential process and legal requirements for referring and identifying students for special education.
learning_objectives: Describe the steps in the pre-referral and referral process.
Understand the timeline and requirements for special education identification.

topics.title: Federal Safeguards of the Rights of Stakeholders
topics.description: Procedural safeguards that protect the rights of students and their families under IDEA.
learning_objectives: Explain procedural safeguards and parental rights under IDEA.
Understand the process of due process, mediation, and informed consent.

topics.title: Components of a Legally Defensible IEP
topics.description: Understanding the required components to create an individualized education program that meets legal standards.
learning_objectives: Identify all legally mandated sections of an Individualized Education Program.
Ensure IEPs are individualized, data-driven, and legally compliant.

topics.title: Major Legislation
topics.description: Examining historical and current federal legislation impacting individuals with disabilities.
learning_objectives: Differentiate the implications of IDEA, Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act, and the ADA.
Understand the historical context of major special education legislation.

topics.title: Roles and Responsibilities of the Special Education Teacher
topics.description: The ethical, legal, and instructional responsibilities unique to special educators.
learning_objectives: Define the ethical and professional responsibilities of special educators.
Outline the case manager role in the special education process.

topics.title: Roles of Other Professionals
topics.description: Understanding the function of related service providers and paraprofessionals.
learning_objectives: Understand the contributions of related service providers (SLPs, OTs, PTs, school psychologists).
Identify the roles of paraprofessionals in delivering special education services.

topics.title: Strengths and Limitations of Collaborative Approaches
topics.description: Best practices for co-teaching, teaming, and consulting across educational environments.
learning_objectives: Implement various co-teaching models (e.g., parallel, station, team teaching).
Evaluate the strengths and limitations of collaborative approaches.

topics.title: Communication with Stakeholders
topics.description: Strategies for clear, responsive, and respectful communication with parents, administrators, and colleagues.
learning_objectives: Facilitate productive parent-teacher conferences and IEP meetings.
Establish ongoing, transparent communication with families and staff.

topics.title: Potential Bias Issues
topics.description: Identifying and managing personal and systemic biases to ensure equitable educational opportunities.
learning_objectives: Recognize implicit biases in educational settings.
Implement culturally responsive teaching practices to counteract bias.

sections.title: Integrated Constructed-response Questions
sections.description: Applying pedagogical knowledge to craft detailed, integrated constructed responses based on classroom scenarios.

topics.title: Instruction and Assessment (Constructed-Response)
topics.description: Practicing constructed responses that link assessment data to targeted instructional planning.
learning_objectives: Analyze a student scenario to propose appropriate instructional strategies.
Justify the selection of assessment tools for a given case study.

topics.title: Learning Environment and Classroom Management (Constructed-Response)
topics.description: Answering constructed-response prompts related to behavioral intervention and environmental design.
learning_objectives: Develop a comprehensive behavior intervention plan (BIP) from a vignette.
Describe modifications to the physical learning environment to support specific needs.

topics.title: Collaboration (Constructed-Response)
topics.description: Formulating detailed responses regarding professional collaboration and family engagement scenarios.
learning_objectives: Outline steps to resolve a collaborative conflict with a general education teacher.
Design a communication plan to engage resistant or overwhelmed parents.
